Prettiest lake in Seoul — stunning solo or date spot. Walk around the lake then explore Lotte World Mall, Lotte Mart and Baekjegobun-ro 43-gil (wine bars, vinyl cafés). @itszdaniitravels pick.
Wine bars, vinyl cafés and cosy restaurants near Seokchon Lake. Beautiful evening/night area. @itszdaniitravels' pick for after the lake walk.
Best club in Hongdae for old school American hip hop and R&B. @itszdaniitravels top pick — also mentions Mongs, Rollie, La Bamba.
One of Seoul's most underrated spots — quiet, relaxing, surrounded by nature. Perfect break from the city. Near Haebangchon.
@itszdaniitravels' favourite restaurant in Seoul — introduces Korean friends to Ethiopian food here. On the Seoul summer bucket list.
Huge vegan menu: vegan rose tteokbokki, vegan mala, vegan jajang, rice balls. Two branches — Ewha and Seongsu. @itszdaniitravels pick.
Street food, hotteok, vintage stores nearby. Browse the area and check out the basketball courts near the river. @itszdaniitravels pick.
Mushroom pesto gimbap with extra pesto sauce — @itszdaniitravels says you MUST try it. Specific dish makes this worth the visit.
Huge lake, beautiful walking paths, cycling tracks, picnic spots. Check the water fountain schedule — super pretty at night. @itszdaniitravels night list.
Walk from Hongdae through Yeonnam-dong — cafés, bakeries, buskers, people sitting outside. Get hotteok by Hongdae subway exit 3. AKA Yeonntral Park.

Place Name: Old Railway Track — Hongdae to Sinchon
Description / Notes: Gorgeous night walk along the old railway track connecting Hongdae to Sinchon. @itszdaniitravels calls it one of the most beautiful spots.
Lively at night — skateboarding area, student energy, bars and clubs. @itszdaniitravels night list entry.
Super popular club in Itaewon for hip hop nights. Mentioned alongside XX2 in Hongdae as main Seoul clubbing options. @itszdaniitravels.
Sugar-filled Korean pancake at Gwangjang Market — 155 reviews, recommended by @chestertravelchingu in the 1-day first-timer Seoul itinerary. Stop between Insadong/Ikseondong and dinner.
Michelin Guide Seoul 2026, 4.39 stars, 16K reviews. Korean food near Gwanghwamun — walk-in waitlist via Tabling app. @chestertravelchingu's dinner rec on the 1-day first-timer Seoul itinerary.
Animal cafe where you can hold and interact with owls. Memorable, low-key experience in Akihabara.
Japanese outdoor/hiking brand - ultra-light jackets, travel clothes, high quality gear. Local favourite, often missed by tourists.
Shrine nestled in a lush forest, ~300 steps up to the Ugeto-iwa Cave at the top. Featured in the anime/film Hotarubi no Mori e. Go early on a weekday to avoid crowds, comfy shoes needed.
Tropical island getaway - beaches, snorkeling, island vibes. Very different pace from mainland Japan.

Iconic cherry blossom mountain in Nara, perfect day trip from Osaka/Kyoto (~2hr train to Yoshino Station + uphill bus). Walk down through layers of sakura - locals do the same walk. ~4hrs at a relaxed pace.
Quiet, emotional, local Japan - the lake that inspired Kimi no Na Wa (Your Name). Go up to Tateishi Park at sunset, bring a snack, and just watch.
One of the best onsen towns in Japan - mountains, cold air, hot water, tradition everywhere. Stay at least one night and try different baths.
Absolutely unreal gorge - turquoise water, dramatic cliffs, mythology. One of the standout Kyushu stops.
UNESCO village - straight out of a fairytale in winter. Snow-covered farmhouses, quiet nights. Stay overnight if possible; once day-trippers leave it's magic.
Some of the best nature in Japan - crystal rivers, alpine views, fresh mountain air. Go early and walk beyond the main bridge for quieter spots.
Old Japan at its best - historic post towns (Tsumago/Magome), forests, and history you can feel walking the old Nakasendo trail.
Custom pillow service by sleep experts - full posture/sleep assessment, test different heights/materials, pillow made on the spot. ¥33,000-44,000. Book a private consultation ahead.
Japanese outerwear engineered with decades of precision - puffer jackets and technical wear. 7 min walk from Meiji-Jingumae Station exit 7.
Down jacket specialist, located in the WAT building near Meiji-Jingumae Station exit 7 (~6 min walk). Pairs well with a Descente visit nearby.
Treasure trove for vintage Rolex and luxury watch hunting - huge selection, English/Mandarin-speaking staff. A must for watch collectors.
Animal cafe with otters - a fun, low-key activity to break up temple-hopping in Kyoto.
Watch wild snow monkeys soak in hot springs - iconic Nagano day trip.
Upscale fashion shopping street near Harajuku - flagship stores and coat/outerwear shopping.
Two weeks here barely scratches the surface - uncrowded alternative to the main Tokyo/Kyoto/Osaka route. Cherry blossoms with no crowds, great for autumn foliage too.
Early 5am wake-up hiking trail - worth it for the sunrise views over Alishan.

Japanese hotel chain right above Ximen Station (exit 2/3). Huge clean rooms, amenities bar in lobby, vacuum-wrap & luggage delivery service, water refill station each floor. Across from Ximending's attractions including The Red House.
"Little Switzerland" - try local Yunnan-inspired dishes, see sheep roaming green meadows with European-style farmhouses. Pairs well with Hehuanshan.
Breathtaking mountain views, standing above the clouds with alpine air. Hike side trails or drive to the top. Best for sunrise - consider staying overnight nearby and renting a car for flexibility.
Niche Asian perfumery - fragrances inspired by familiar flavours alongside scents you won't find elsewhere. Must-visit for niche perfume fans.

Misty forests, 1000+ year old cypress trees, the iconic Alishan Forest Railway, sea of clouds, and fairytale sunrise views. Entrance ~NT$300. Stay overnight to beat the crowds and get the full atmosphere - HSR to Chiayi then bus/railway, or bus direct from Chiayi Station (~2.5hrs).
Stop on a 48hr Yilan itinerary - local farm experience.

Place Name: Su Ao Fish Port (蘇澳漁港)
Description / Notes: Fresh sashimi lunch straight off the boats at this fishing port.
Quick cliffside stop near Su'ao - distinctive tofu-block-shaped rock formations along the coast.
Black sand beach stop on the Yilan coastal route.
Popular night market to cap off a Yilan day trip.
Capital city and great intro to Kyrgyzstan — local museums and try beshbarmak at Navat restaurant.
3-4 day horseback trek — immersive nomadic experience. Loop via Kyzart: ride to lake, cold dip, yurt with log burner, back through stunning mountains. ★ top pick.
Tags: horseback,yurt stay,alpine lake,nomadic experience,★ top pick

Description / Notes: Starting point for Song-Kul horseback treks. Multi-course lunch with lots of tea at the stables on arrival.
Kyrgyzstan's largest lake. Look out for eagle hunting demonstrations.
Hot springs valley near Karakol — a must-visit. Often combined with Ala-Kul trek.
High-altitude turquoise lake near Karakol, frequently paired with Altyn Arashan. ★ top pick.
Remote, treacherous journey — plan 3 full days. One of the most striking and least-visited alpine lakes in the country.
